title: तिरुप्पावै
devanagariText:
॥ श्री तिरुप्पावै ॥

॥ आण्डाल् कृतम् ॥

मार्गळि त्तिङ्गळ् मदि निरैन्द नन्नाळाल्
नीराड प्पोदुवीर् पोदुमिनो नेरिळैयीर्
शीर् मल्गुम् आय्प्पाडि शेल्वच् चिरुमीर्गाळ्
कूर्वेल् कोडुन्दोळिलन् नन्दगोपन् कुमरन्
एरार्न्द कण्णि यशोदै इळञ्शिङ्गम्
कार्मेनिच् चेङ्गण ् कदिर् मदियम् पोल् मुगत्तान्
नाराय णने नमक्के परै तरुवान्
पारोर् पुगळ प्पडिन्देलोर् एम्पावाय्॥ १॥

वैय त्तु वाळ्वीर्गाळ् नामुम् नम् पावैक्कु
शेय्युम् किरिशैगळ् केळीरो पार्कडलुळ्
पैय त्तुयिन्र परमन् अडिपाडि
नेय्य ुण्णोम् पालुण्णोम् नाट्काले नीराडि
मैय िट्टेळुदोम् मलरिट्टु नाम् मुडियोम्
शेय्यादन शेय्योम् तीक्कुरळै चेन्रोदोम्
ऐय्यमुम् पिच्चैयुम् आन्दनैयुम् कैक्काट्टि
उय्युमारेण्णि उगन्देलोर् एम्पावाय्॥ २॥

ओङ्गि उलगळन्द उत्तमन् पेर्पाडि
नाङ्गळ् नम् पावैक्कुच् चाट्रि नीराडिनाल्
तीङ्गिन्रि नाडेल्लाम् तिङ्गळ् मुम्मारि पेय्दु
ओङ्गु शेञ्शालि पडुदिरुन्दु कालत्ते
पूङ्गुवळै पोदिल् पोरिवण्डु कण्पडुप्प
तेङ्गादे पुक्किरुन्दु शीर्त्तमुलै पट्रि
वाङ्गक् कुडम् निरैक्कुम् वळ्ळल् पेरुम् पशुक्कळ्
नीङ्गाद शेल्वम् निरैन्देलोर् एम्पावाय्॥ ३॥

आळि मळैक्कण्णा ओन्रु नी कैकरवेल्
आळियुळ् पुक्कु मुगन्दु कोडार्तेरि
ऊळि मुदल्वन् उरुवम् पोल् मेय्करुत्तु
पाळियन्दोळुडे पर्पनाबन् कैयिल्
आळि पोल् मिन्नि वलम्बुरि पोल् निन्रदिरिन्दु
ताळादे शार्ङ्गमुदैत्त शरमळै पोल्
वाळ वुलगिनिल् पेय्दिडाय् नाङ्गळुम्
मार्गळि नीराड मगिळ्न्देलोर् एम्पावाय्॥ ४॥

मायनै मन्नु वडमदुरै मैन्दनै
तूय पेरुनीर् यमुनैत् तुरैवनै
आयर् कुलत्तिनिल् तोन्रुम् अणि विळक्कै
तायैक् कुडल् विळक्कम् शेय्द दामोदरनै
तूयोमाय् वन्दु नाम् तूमलर् तूवित्तोळुदु
वायिनाल् पाडि मनत्तिनाल् शिन्दिक्क
पोय पिळैयुम् पुगुदरुवान् निन्रनवुम्
तीयिनिल् तूशागुम् शेप्पेलोर् एम्पावाय्॥ ५॥

॥ इति श्री आण्डाल् विरचितम् तिरुप्पावै सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Thiruppavai ||

|| Andal Kritam ||

Margali Thingal Madi Nirainda Nannalal
Nirada Poduvir Podumino Nerilaiyir
Sir Malgum Aippadi Selvach Chirumirgal
Kurvel Kodunthozilan Nandagopan Kumaran
Erarndha Kanni Yashodai Ilam Singam
Karmenich Chengan Kadhir Madhiyam Pol Mugathan
Narayane Namakke Parai Taruvan
Paror Pugala Padindelor Empaavai || 1 ||

Vaiyattu Valvirgal Namum Nam Pavaikku
Seyyum Kirisaigal Keliro Parkadalul
Paiya Thuyindra Paraman Adipadi
Neyyunnom Palunnom Natkaale Niradi
Maiyittezhudom Malarittu Nam Mudiyom
Seyyadana Seyyom Thikkuralai Sendrodom
Aiyyamum Pichchaiyum Aandanaiyum Kaikatti
Uyyumaarenni Ugandelor Empaavai || 2 ||

Ongi Ulagalanda Uttaman Perpadi
Nangal Nam Pavaikkuch Chatri Niradiinal
Thingindri Nadellam Thingal Mummari Peydu
Ongu Senchali Paduthirundhu Kaalatthe
Pungkuvalai Podil Porivandu Kanpadup
Thengade Pukkirundhu Sirttamulai Patri
Vanga Kudam Niraikkum Vallal Perum Pasukkal
Ningada Selvam Niraindelor Empaavai || 3 ||

Aali Malaikanna Ondru Ni Kaikaravel
Aaliyul Pukku Mugandu Kodaartheri
Oozhi Mudalvan Uruvam Pol Meykaruttu
Paaliyandolude Parpanaban Kaiyil
Aali Pol Minni Valamburi Pol Nindradirindhu
Thaalade Sarangam Udaitta Saramalai Pol
Vaala Ulaginil Peydidaay Nangalum
Margali Nirada Magilndelor Empaavai || 4 ||

Maayanai Mannu Vadamadurai Maindanai
Thooya Perunir Yamunai Thuraivanai
Aayar Kulatthinil Thondrum Ani Vilakkai
Thaayaik Kudal Vilakkam Seyda Damodaranai
Thooyomai Vandhu Nam Thumalar Thuvittholludhu
Vaayinal Padi Manattinal Sindhikka
Poya Pilaiyum Pugudharuvan Nindranavum
Thiyinil Thusaagum Seppelor Empaavai || 5 ||

|| Iti Shri Andal Virachitam Thiruppavai Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्री आण्डाल् द्वारा रचित तिरुप्पावै तमिल भक्ति साहित्य का सर्वोत्कृष्ट ग्रन्थ है। मार्गशीर्ष (मार्गळि) मास में गोपियों द्वारा श्रीकृष्ण की भक्ति में गाए गए ३० पाशुरम् (छन्दों) का यह संकलन है।

प्रथम पाशुरम्: मार्गळि मास के शुभ दिनों में स्नान करने चलो हे सखियों! नन्दगोप के पुत्र, यशोदा के सिंह शावक, सुन्दर नेत्रों वाले, चन्द्र-सूर्य सदृश मुख वाले नारायण हमें वर देंगे।

द्वितीय पाशुरम्: इस लोक में रहने वालों! हमारे व्रत के नियम सुनो - क्षीरसागर में शयन करने वाले परमात्मा के चरण गाकर, हम घी-दूध का त्याग करेंगी, प्रातःकाल स्नान करेंगी।

तृतीय पाशुरम्: तीनों लोकों को नापने वाले उत्तम पुरुष का नाम गाकर स्नान करने से देश में अच्छी वर्षा होगी, धान की फसल लहलहाएगी।

यह ग्रन्थ श्रीवैष्णव सम्प्रदाय में अत्यन्त पवित्र माना जाता है और मार्गशीर्ष मास में प्रतिदिन पाठ किया जाता है।
benefits: Supreme devotional text of Shri Vaishnava tradition recited in Margazhi month
Composed by Andal, one of the 12 Alvars, expressing intense love for Krishna
Traditionally associated with divine grace of lord krishna and sri ranganatha
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires when recited daily during margazhi (margashirsha) month
Invokes moksha and eternal service to the lord
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Krishna, Thiruppavai (तिरुप्पावै) stands as a sacred prayer. Attributed to Nalayira Divya Prabandham (Andal), this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to supreme devotional text of Shri Vaishnava tradition recited in Margazhi month as well as bring divine grace of Lord Krishna and Sri Ranganatha. Additionally, it is known to fulfill all desires when recited daily during Margazhi (Margashirsha) month. This sacred text is ideally recited on Daily and during Margashirsha Month and Vaikuntha Ekadashi for enhanced blessings. With 30 verses and a reading time of about 15 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Thiruppavai in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Thiruppavai by Andal in Devanagari with Hindi meaning. Sacred 30 verses of Krishna bhakti for Margazhi month recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Thiruppavai by Andal?
seoFaqs.answer: The Thiruppavai (Tamil: திருப்பாவை, तिरुप्पावै — Sacred Vow) is a celebrated 30-pasuram (verse) Tamil devotional poem composed by Andal (also called Goda Devi or Kothai), the only female Alvar among the twelve Alvar saints of South Indian Vaishnavism (c. 8th-9th century CE). The thirty pasurams are framed as the Margali-vrata of the gopis of Vrindavan: Andal imagines herself and her companions as gopis observing a sacred vow during the Tamil month of Margazhi (Sanskrit Margashirsha, mid-December to mid-January) — bathing in the Yamuna at dawn, going from house to house waking the sleeping gopis, and finally reaching the chamber of Krishna to ask for the parai (the divine drum / boon of devotional service). The Thiruppavai is part of the Nalayira Divya Prabandham — the 4000 sacred Tamil hymns of the twelve Alvars — and is regarded as the supreme pasuram-collection of Sri Vaishnavism. (Source: Nalayira Divya Prabandham, Andal corpus.)

seoFaqs.question: Who was Andal and what is her story?
seoFaqs.answer: Andal (Tamil: ஆண்டாள், the One Who Rules) was the foster-daughter of Vishnuchitta Periyalvar, the Vaishnava poet-saint of Srivilliputhur (in present-day Tamil Nadu), who found her as an infant girl in the tulasi garden of his temple. She was named Kothai (garland-giver) and grew up immersed in devotion to Vatapatra-Sayi, the form of Vishnu enshrined in her father's temple. Tradition records that she would daily wear the flower-garlands prepared for the deity before they were offered, declaring she would marry no one but Lord Ranganatha of Srirangam. The Lord himself confirmed in a dream to the Srirangam Bhattar (priest) that Andal was His chosen bride; she was carried in a procession to Srirangam where she merged into the deity at the age of fifteen. She is therefore called Andal — She Who Rules (over the Lord Himself) — and is the only female saint to be enshrined as a deity in Vaishnava temples worldwide. (Source: traditional Sri Vaishnava hagiography; Periyalvar tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Thiruppavai recited and what is the Margali Vrata?
seoFaqs.answer: The Thiruppavai is recited daily through the entire Tamil month of Margazhi (Sanskrit Margashirsha, c. 16 December to 13 January) — one pasuram on each of the thirty days, beginning on the first day of Margazhi and concluding on Bhogi (the day before Pongal). This is the Margali-vrata: pre-dawn bathing (often in cold water), parikrama of the village or temple while singing the day's pasuram, special offerings of sakkarai pongal (sweet rice) and akkaravadisil (milk-rice) at the temple, observance of brahmacharya, ahimsa and truthfulness, and abstention from butter, milk, kohl and ornaments — the conditions Andal herself describes in pasuram 2. The Krishna-mentioned-by-name Bhagavad Gita verse Masanam Margashirsho-aham (10.35) — Among months I am Margashirsha — sanctifies this month for Krishna-bhakti. The full 30-day recitation in every Sri Vaishnava temple of South India is one of the largest synchronised devotional events in the world. (Sources: Sri Vaishnava sampradaya; Bhagavad Gita 10.35.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the famous opening pasuram Margali Thingal Madi Nirainda?
seoFaqs.answer: The first pasuram of the Thiruppavai opens the entire Margali vrata: Margali Thingal Madi Nirainda Nannalal, Nirada Poduvir Podumino Nerilaiyir — In the auspicious days of the full-moon month of Margali, you who would bathe (in the sacred Yamuna), come, come, O bejewelled maidens! Andal then calls out to the gopis: O wealthy children of the prosperous Aippadi (Gokula) of cowherds! O daughters! Nandagopa's son — the spear-bearer of fierce action, the lion-cub of doe-eyed Yashoda, of dark cloud-complexion, with red lotus eyes, with face like the sun and moon — that very Narayana will give us the parai (boon). Sing of His glory, O world, and bathe! The pasuram establishes the entire frame of the Thiruppavai: the gopis as Andal and her companions, Krishna as the boon-giver, and the Margali bathing as the path to the parai — the supreme service of God. (Source: Andal, Thiruppavai pasuram 1.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the parai that Andal asks Krishna for in the Thiruppavai?
seoFaqs.answer: The parai (Tamil for ceremonial drum) is the central object of the Margali vrata in the Thiruppavai — the gopis tell Krishna again and again Parai taruvan (give us the parai). Outwardly, the parai is the festive drum given to the village by the king to celebrate a successful vrata. Inwardly, the parai is the supreme boon — the eternal kainkarya (loving service) to the Lord and His devotees in the spiritual world (Srivaikuntha). Sri Vaishnava acharyas (especially Ramanuja and Vedanta Desika in their commentaries on the Thiruppavai) interpret the parai as moksha understood as eternal nitya-kainkarya — not merely liberation from samsara, but the privilege of perpetual service to Sriman Narayana. The closing pasuram (30) clarifies: Inru Iru Tirumagal Iniya Nayakanay — May we serve forever the Lord with the two Lakshmis (Sri-Bhumi), having come from the Margali vrata. The parai is therefore Andal's name for prapatti — total surrender unto the Lord. (Sources: Sri Vaishnava acharya commentaries on Thiruppavai.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does daily recitation of the Thiruppavai grant?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition assigns the following blessings to sincere recitation of the Thiruppavai during Margazhi (or daily through the year): (1) the merit of Andal's own bridal-bhakti — being adopted by the Lord as His own bride, since reciting Andal's words is the highest form of identifying with Andal, (2) success in finding a worthy life-partner — Andal is the supreme deity invoked by unmarried girls for vivaha-prapti, (3) prosperity, wealth (sampat) and family welfare — the Margali-vrata is described as bringing rains, abundant harvests and overflowing milk-pots in pasuram 3, (4) prema-bhakti — the awakening of madhura-bhava, the bridal mood of devotion, (5) protection from sins through Krishna-smarana, (6) eligibility for nitya-kainkarya in Srivaikuntha — the eternal service which is the parai itself, (7) cure of long-standing afflictions through the Lord's grace as Govinda, and (8) admission into the Sri Vaishnava parampara of bhakti. (Sources: Sri Vaishnava tradition; Andal's own phala-shruti pasuram 30.)
